description: Content-scoped contract bits moved OUT of base.contract.json by the Swift 2.4 base split (base 3.0.0 framework-only). surface-swift owns the Swift content surface: the Swift 2 content area (area 3, EN), the entire merge tree, UrlPath, and its own item-type XMLs (itemtypes/, from the official Swift v2.4.0 design package) so the surface is self-contained.

urlPathOwnership.decision: UrlPath ships in surface-swift (replace/_sql/UrlPath), NOT in base 3.0.0.
urlPathOwnership.rationale: The table's single row is a 301 friendly-URL redirect (products-* -> Default.aspx?ID=50) bound to UrlPathAreaId 3 and a Swift page id. Friendly URLs resolve against pages; a framework-only base ships no areas and no pages, so a base-owned UrlPath row would dangle and force base re-proves on Swift page churn. It travels with the content it routes.

navDepth.note: Menu-bar affordance obligation (RUN-NAV-DEFAULT): the header nav needs >=1 menu-visible child relation for the theme affordance probes to exercise dropdown depth. Owned by the Swift content tree (surface-swift); the gate's Ensure-NavDepth authors the minimal depth when absent.

titleRules.note: Title-integrity contract (gate Step 10c2): for every item-typed page, DB item Title == YAML fields.Title. Applies to this surface's replace/_content tree.

perEnvironmentAreaExclusions.note: Per-environment Area configuration deliberately NOT serialized by the base (LRN-08). These Area columns/fields are excluded by config/swift-2.3.json (the 'Site framework' deploy predicate's excludeAreaColumns + excludeFields), so the base ships brand/content-neutral structure only and every consuming environment owns these values. A fresh deserialize therefore leaves them at whatever the target DB already holds - it does NOT overwrite them.

consumerObligation.bindSiteRoot: After deserialize, set each area's AreaFrontpage to the environment's site-root page id.
consumerObligation.bindShop: After deserialize, set each area's AreaEcomShopId to the environment shop. The base ships exactly one shop: SHOP1 ('B2B Commerce Store').
consumerObligation.restart: Restart the DW host after binding - Area routing, frontpage resolution, and shop resolution are cached at host startup.

protectedItemTypes.note: Upstream DW/Swift identifiers - NEVER renamed by the vocabulary rewrite (plan section 3.1). PK tables are ItemType_<systemName> (hyphenated on DW 10.26.9). (Swift list owned by surface-swift since the base split; Headless list owned by surface-headless.)
